sources.label: Globe and Mail - AECOM declares quarterly dividend (Jun 3, pre-window)
Simply Wall St - AECOM valuation after defense and Singapore wins (pre-window)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6 and today's -0.4% is drift; yesterday's down-move already reconciled rank 76 with the adjusted score and YTD damage. The -27% YTD washout with the permitting/power-design thesis intact marginally improves asymmetry, worth +1 drift but not a rank change one day later.
verdicts.bull: Engineering and environmental-permitting capacity is a genuinely scarce input for data-center and grid buildouts, and ACM trades near 52-week lows at ~$9B cap with insider buying and no insider selling - cheap optionality on the bottleneck.
verdicts.neutral: AECOM is large and diversified, so AI/data-center exposure is a small slice of revenue; the recent wins (Canada defense list, Singapore waste JV) are real but not AI-supply-chain catalysts, leaving it a low-purity expression of the thesis.
verdicts.bear: The -27% YTD reflects broader E&C demand and margin concerns; if infrastructure/services spending softens further, the data-center services sliver cannot carry a $9B diversified consultancy, and the stock keeps de-rating.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A named hyperscaler or utility-scale data-center campus design/permitting award, or Q3 FY26 results showing data-center/power backlog acceleration.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Diversified-services dilution: AI exposure stays too small to matter while the core E&C cycle and government spending mix continue to drag estimates lower.
